library_preparation_preflight.summary: The Jackson module is an add-on whose POM marks jsonschema-generator and Jackson APIs as provided; meaningful tests need the aligned generator dependency available.

deterministic_setup.reason:
jsonschema-module-jackson implements the jsonschema-generator Module API and normal documented usage passes JacksonModule into SchemaGeneratorConfigBuilder; the module POM declares jsonschema-generator and Jackson APIs with provided scope, so the module artifact alone is not enough for compile/runti
...[truncated]

library_preparation_preflight.agent_guidance: No Docker image, environment variable, or system property setup appears necessary. Generated tests should exercise the documented integration path by registering JacksonModule with SchemaGeneratorConfigBuilder/SchemaGenerator and generating schemas for small annotated fixture classes using Jackson annotations such as @JsonProperty, @JsonIgnore, @JsonPropertyDescription, @JsonTypeInfo/@JsonSubTypes, or @JsonUnwrapped.
risks: If the harness already injects provided-scope dependencies differently, the extra dependency may be redundant, but it matches documented usage and version-alignment guidance.
Coverage can remain superficial if tests only instantiate JacksonModule without generating a schema from annotated model classes.
